Subject: SDT paper + ways to collaborate with Careerspan

Hi Shujaat,

Really enjoyed the conversation. Your “myth-busting” cadence and the focus on human judgment with AI line up closely with our work. The frame that stuck with me was the “base athleticism” of soft skills—adaptability, learning-how-to-learn, and connection—as the substrate for everything else.

Proposed next steps
- I’ll send over the self‑determination theory (SDT) paper and a short primer on why it maps to career motivation and skill adoption (esp. for PMs).  [ref: #/ctas/0, #/ctas/2]
- Two collaboration paths to explore:
  1) A weekly “AI & Careers” myth‑busting series co‑developed with Careerspan’s PM community (grounded in real cases, not hot takes).  [ref: #/topics/2]
  2) A soft‑skills “base athleticism” toolkit for early PMs (communication, learning loops, relationship leverage) with quick wins and a longer arc.  [ref: #/topics/1]
- We can also share involvement tiers/options so you can pick the scope that fits.  [ref: #/ctas/4]

If you’re up for it, could we schedule 30 minutes next week to align on which path to pilot first? I’ll send the SDT doc immediately and a 1‑pager on the two options above.
